entry.title: RAP: Numbering | Early numbering | Late numbering | Managed numbering
entry.content:
Numbering

 

Road Map for Learning Numbering in RAP

 

 

Early numbering:

The numbering type early numbering refers to an early value assignment for the primary key field. In this case, the final key value is available in the transactional buffer instantly after the MODIFY request for CREATE.

 

1.  External Early Numbering:

  • consumer provides key
  • Key should be unique
  • key should be editable in create, and non-editable in modify. syntax:

 

2.  Managed Internal Early Numbering:

  • Managed early numbering is only possible for key fields with ABAP type raw (16) (UUID) of BOs with

implementation type managed.

  • UUID is read-only. syntax:

 

3.  Unmanaged Internal Early Numbering:

  • Unmanaged Early Numbering is available in Managed and Unmanaged Implementation Scenarios with Draft Capabilities
  • Key is read-only. Syntax:

Bdef:

Local handler class:

 

Late numbering:

  • The RAP runtime engine assigns values to the primary key fields.
  • In the ABAP behaviour pool, the RAP saver method adjust_numbers must be implemented to assign a final primary key value for each entity instance.
  • The key value for an instance is assigned just before the instance is saved on the database. Thus, a gapless assignment of unique keys is ensured.
  • Late numbering is available for managed and unmanaged implementation scenarios with and without draft.

Note:

  • late numbering can't be external, since a consumer can't influence the SAVE sequence after the point of no return.
  • If draft enabled, it is mandatory that the RAP draft table has an additional key field DRAFTUUID of data type raw (16).

 

Summary:

  • passed externally by the application user: External numbering
  • set internally by the framework: Managed internal
  • set internally by developer (implementation the FOR-NUMBERING method): Unmanaged Internal

entry.title: Re: Relationship between space and Business Catalog
entry.content: If an app tile is included in the Page but not in the corresponding catalog, then the tile will neither be visible on the Page in the runtime, nor on the App Finder page.

entry.title: First Half 2025 Release: Innovations galore in SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll (ECP)
entry.content:
Hello Everyone! I am very excited to share with you all the innovations we are delivering in the first half 2025 release for our favorite “SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll” (ECP) solution. Some of these are already being called “game changing” innovations in the marketplace. You will be thrilled to see that SAP’s product and engineering teams have kept the promises it made during SuccessConnect roadmap and strategy sessions in Lisbon last October. Have a look at the for the full release notes and all of the details for everything we are delivering.  

 

AI-enabled “Explain my pay statement” capability via Joule

I am proud to introduce the “Explain My Pay statement” capability. Now, employees can easily understand details of their pay statements by simply asking Joule, SAP’s AI copilot. For example, requests like compare my historical pay statements, explain a specific deduction and other amounts on a payslip. This reduces HR inquiries and tickets, and boosts employee satisfaction. The replies by Joule will be based on the company’s policy documents. And by the way, Joule is now also available on mobile making it even more convenient to get your work done anywhere, at any time.

For more information on this feature see the “What’s New Viewer”


Explain my pay statement feature: Prompt “Compare my payslip from last month to this month”

 


Explain my pay statement feature: Prompt “why is my deduction so high”

 

Enhanced Payroll Control Center (PCC) for admins

Secondly, I am happy to share our Enhanced Payroll Control Center through which business users can now manage a complete payroll cycle from start to end, not just running the payroll calculation, but all payroll activities that are necessary. We offer easy to configure payroll process flow and dashboards without IT skills which streamlines operations.

This significantly enhanced user experience provides real-time insights from pre-payroll activities -  such as gathering time and attendance records, collecting benefits and deductions information, to post-payroll activities such as bank transfer, statutory reporting, and internal reporting.

For more information on this feature see the “What’s New Viewer”


Complete payroll cycle view from start to end

 


Enhanced Payroll Control Center dashboard

 

Position Budgeting Control (PBC) for cloud Integration with Employee Central Payroll

Thirdly, we are thrilled to announce the integration of the Position Budgeting Control Workbench with ECP. Combined with the existing integrations of SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central and SAP S/4 HANA cloud, this innovation provides live insights into personnel budget utilization and costs. This enables better management of allocated funds and grants, giving full visibility of salary costs, committed budget and available funds for personnel expenses especially in regulated industries such as Public Sector, Education and Research, healthcare and more.

For more information on this feature see the “What’s New Viewer”

 

Access to payroll information for terminated employees (alumni)

Our offboarding solution now allows terminated employees and company alumni to access payroll information (for example, pay statements or tax documents for a specified time after the termination date). This avoids manual effort for payroll administrators to provide documents to terminated employees.

For more information on this feature see the “What’s New Viewer”

 

New Local Version – Bulgaria

With this release we are excited to now provide the ability to run SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll for Bulgaria. This now brings our localized versions to 53 native locales, highest in the industry. (See the full list on our localization site with a cool interactive map). Native local country versions support our global base of customers with a local, compliant, and proven payroll platform. We also have five additional partner countries available as described in SAP KBA 3031216 - Employee Central Payroll: Availability of Partner Local Versions. EPI-USE has localized Peru, Vietnam, Denmark, Namibia, and Angola.

 

Our strategy is to deliver local versions we offered on-premise based upon customer and prospect demand so more to come for localizations. We also have a high interest from various partners to continue to localize more versions.

For more information on this feature see the “What’s New Viewer”

What Else is New this Release?

There are a number of additional innovations available for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll in the first half 2025 release, including

  • Joule Available in SAP SuccessFactors Mobile
  • Enhancements to the Latest People Profile in SAP SuccessFactors Mobile
  • Replication of Dependents for Malaysia
  • Report for Checking and Creating Jobs, Positions, and Organizational Units Available for Chile
  • And more

See the “” for more information on these features.

Where Can I Find More Information?

Here are additional resources for those interested in learning more about SAP Payroll solutions

  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Website
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll SAP Help (Implementation) (Requires ID)
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Community Page (Discussion Forum) (Requires ID)
  • SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll Learning Journey (Educational Content) (Requires SAP Lea...
  • Other recent release highlights for SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central Payroll
    • 2H/2024 Release Highlight Blog
    • 1H/2024 Release Highlight Blog
    • 2H/2023 Release Highlight Blog
    • 1H/2023 Release Highlight Blog 

For information on release highlights across SAP SuccessFactors HCM, visit this one stop shop for all product release and road map information

entry.title: Leveraging AI-Driven Test Automation for SAP Commerce Cloud Optimization
entry.content:
Why SAP Commerce Cloud Testing?

With the growing adoption of SAP Commerce Cloud for e-commerce, maintaining system performance and reliability has become critical. Due to the platform’s complexity—frequent updates, integrations, and diverse user requirements—SAP Commerce Cloud testing becomes increasingly critical.

With SAP Commerce Cloud's numerous integrations, thorough testing is needed to guarantee seamless functionality across all components. And, as e-commerce operations scale, the demand for continuous, efficient testing grows bigger still.

Consequently, manual testing is no longer sufficient to keep pace with these evolving demands. However, AI-driven SAP Commerce Cloud testing offers a solution. By leveraging AI, businesses can optimize platform performance, reduce time-to-market, and enhance overall reliability while ensuring a high-quality user experience.

The Challenges of SAP Commerce Cloud Testing

Traditional testing methods often fall short when it comes to handling the scale, complexity, and rapid changes associated with SAP Commerce Cloud environments. Some of the most prevalent challenges are:

High Volume of Transactions: E-commerce platforms handle a massive volume of transactions, making manual or conventional testing impractical.

Frequent Updates: Regular updates, customizations, and feature enhancements in the SAP Commerce Cloud demand frequent testing to ensure zero disruptions to services.

Complex Integrations: SAP Commerce Cloud integrates with numerous third-party systems like payment gateways, CRM tools, and inventory management systems, which increases testing complexity.

User Experience: Ensuring a consistent and bug-free user experience across a wide range of devices, browsers, and networks requires thorough testing at scale.

Regression Testing: With the constant deployment of new features, regression testing is necessary to ensure that updates do not break existing functionalities.

These challenges require a robust, scalable, and efficient testing solution capable of adapting to the evolving needs of modern e-commerce platforms.

Enter AI-Driven Test Automation

AI is revolutionizing how organizations approach testing, offering new opportunities for optimizing SAP Commerce Cloud environments. AI-driven test automation leverages machine learning, natural language processing (NLP), and other advanced algorithms to significantly improve the efficiency and accuracy of testing processes. Unlike traditional methods, which rely on predefined scripts and manual intervention, AI-based testing solutions can autonomously identify issues, predict potential failure points, and adapt to new changes in real time.

How AI-Driven Test Automation Works

AI-powered test automation for SAP Commerce Cloud works through several key features that make it highly effective:

Intelligent Test Generation: AI tools have the capability to autonomously create test cases by analyzing application behavior and leveraging historical testing data. By analyzing past test outcomes and learning from them, AI systems can create tests that cover a broader range of scenarios, including edge cases that might otherwise be missed.

Dynamic Test Maintenance: As SAP Commerce Cloud environments evolve, traditional test scripts often require manual updates. AI-driven solutions can autonomously update tests when the application undergoes changes or when new features are introduced, reducing the overhead of test maintenance.

Enhanced Test Coverage: AI-powered testing can automatically detect gaps in test coverage by analyzing the system architecture and usage patterns. This ensures that all critical functionalities are tested, regardless of how frequently the platform changes.

Regression Testing: One of the most time-consuming aspects of testing is regression testing, which ensures that new changes do not negatively impact existing functionality. AI-based automation tools can intelligently prioritize regression tests based on the likelihood of change, focusing resources on areas of the application most at risk of failure.

Faster Execution and Feedback: AI-driven test automation tools are capable of executing tests at a significantly faster rate than manual testing. This speed is crucial for organizations looking to deploy frequent updates or rapidly respond to customer feedback.

Predictive Analytics: AI solutions can predict areas of the SAP Commerce Cloud platform that are most likely to fail or require attention, based on historical test data. This proactive strategy allows businesses to address potential issues before they affect users.

Key Benefits of AI-Driven Test Automation for SAP Commerce Cloud Optimization

The integration of AI into SAP Commerce Cloud testing offers several compelling benefits that directly contribute to platform optimization:

1. Faster Time-to-Market

AI-driven test automation accelerates the testing process, allowing businesses to deploy new features and updates faster. This is crucial in an e-commerce landscape where speed and agility are key differentiators. By reducing the time spent on manual testing and troubleshooting, organizations can roll out innovations quicker, gaining a competitive edge.

2. Cost Savings

Although the upfront cost of AI-driven testing tools may appear high, the long-term savings are considerable. By automating repetitive and time-consuming tasks, businesses can reduce the need for manual testing resources. Furthermore, the reduced risk of bugs or system failures means fewer emergency fixes, leading to lower operational costs.

3. Improved Test Accuracy

AI systems learn from past tests and continuously improve their testing strategies, leading to more accurate and comprehensive testing. This reduces the likelihood of bugs slipping through the cracks and ensures that any issues are caught early in the development cycle.

4. Scalability

As businesses scale their SAP Commerce Cloud environments to handle higher volumes of traffic and transactions, AI-driven testing can easily adapt to the increased complexity. These tools are designed to scale with the platform, ensuring consistent performance testing regardless of the size of the e-commerce operation.

5. Seamless Integration with CI/CD Pipelines

AI-based test automation tools integrate seamlessly with CI/CD pipelines, allowing businesses to implement automated testing at every stage of the software development lifecycle. This ensures that testing becomes a seamless part of the development process, reducing bottlenecks and improving overall workflow efficiency.

6. Enhanced User Experience

Ultimately, the goal of testing is to deliver a flawless user experience. By using AI to identify issues early and test across a wide range of devices and platforms, businesses can ensure that customers enjoy a smooth, bug-free experience across all touchpoints.

Real-World Applications of AI-Driven Test Automation in SAP Commerce Cloud

Leading organizations are already leveraging AI-driven test automation to optimize their SAP Commerce Cloud platforms. For example, companies in the retail and manufacturing sectors have deployed AI-based testing tools to streamline the testing of customer-facing websites, ensuring a seamless shopping experience across multiple channels. Similarly, organizations in the financial services industry are using AI-driven testing to validate integrations with back-end systems, such as payment processors and CRM tools, ensuring that all parts of the SAP Commerce Cloud ecosystem work together without issues.

In addition, AI-based solutions can help improve accessibility testing for customers with disabilities, ensuring compliance with legal requirements and enhancing the overall customer experience.

Conclusion

The adoption of SAP Commerce Cloud provides businesses with powerful tools to enhance their e-commerce operations, but ensuring optimal performance across complex environments requires robust and efficient testing strategies. AI-driven test automation offers a cutting-edge solution for overcoming the challenges of traditional testing methods, enabling organizations to accelerate their time-to-market, reduce costs, and deliver a superior user experience.

By leveraging AI to automate and optimize the testing process, businesses can ensure that their SAP Commerce Cloud platforms remain reliable, scalable, and capable of meeting the demands of modern consumers. With continuous advancements in AI and machine learning, the future of SAP Commerce Cloud testing is not just about detecting errors but predicting and preventing them—leading to more innovative and successful e-commerce solutions.

entry.title: Generative AI: The Key to Streamlined SAP S/4HANA Migration Planning
entry.content:
Generative AI: The Key to Streamlined SAP S/4HANA Migration Planning

Introduction
Migrating from SAP ECC to SAP S/4HANA is not just a technical upgrade—it’s a bold step toward modernizing business processes and unlocking capabilities that prepare organizations for the future. Despite its transformative potential, this journey comes with challenges: managing legacy systems, allocating resources effectively, and mitigating risks are just a few. Generative AI is changing the game. By bringing automation, insights, and predictability to migration planning, this advanced AI technology enables businesses to move forward with precision and confidence.

Revolutionizing Migration Planning with Generative AI

Generative AI has the unique ability to streamline complex processes and solve migration challenges. Here are the keyways it can make a difference:

  • Automated System Analysis
    Generative AI examines the existing ECC landscape with precision. It scans for legacy customizations, identifies redundant or outdated configurations, and highlights areas for optimization. This process provides businesses with an accurate understanding of their system without the need for time-consuming manual efforts.
  • Proactive Risk Mitigation
    By analyzing past migration projects and leveraging predictive models, generative AI identifies potential risks ahead of time. Whether it's data compatibility issues or bottlenecks during deployment, organizations can use these insights to prevent disruptions and ensure smoother migrations.
  • Optimized Resource Allocation
    Generative AI evaluates organizational resources like budgets, personnel, and timelines. Based on this analysis, it provides recommendations for efficient resource allocation, ensuring critical elements of the migration are prioritized without wasted effort.
  • Scenario Simulations for Strategic Planning
    Organizations can utilize generative AI to simulate different migration approaches—whether it's phased rollouts, big-bang migrations, or hybrid models. These simulations help decision-makers weigh the advantages and drawbacks of each strategy, ensuring the migration plan aligns perfectly with business objectives.
  • Enhanced Decision-Making
    Real-time insights from generative AI empower project teams to make informed decisions. By continuously analyzing migration progress and flagging areas for improvement, AI ensures agility and alignment throughout the transition process.

A Real-World Success Story
One example is a company leveraging generative AI during its migration from ECC to S/4HANA. AI tools helped the organization assess custom legacy components and identify incompatibilities with S/4HANA. Recommendations were provided to streamline their roadmap, resulting in reduced downtime and faster deployment timelines—all without sacrificing system integrity.

Future Trends: AI’s Growing Role in ERP Transformation
Generative AI is far from reaching its full potential. Its integration with tools like SAP’s Business Technology Platform (BTP) is paving the way for even more advanced solutions, including predictive analytics and intelligent automation. As businesses prepare for the future, AI’s role in ERP transformations will continue to grow, offering faster, smarter migration journeys.

Conclusion
Generative AI is not just a tool; it’s a strategic enabler for ERP modernization. By automating system analysis, optimizing resources, and minimizing risks, this technology empowers organizations to approach the transition to S/4HANA with confidence and clarity. As the deadline for ECC support approaches, embracing generative AI could be the difference between a complicated migration and a seamless transformation.

I will soon be detailing on each point with an individual blog.

entry.title: BTP Neo to Cloud Foundry Migration
entry.content:
Dear Integration Experts,

As we are aware that SAP Neo is going to be sunset on December 31, 2028, and there are many projects which are running on Neo and needs to be migrated to Multicloud environment or Cloud Foundry. I am writing this blog based on my real time experience about migration.

The multi-cloud foundation has become the strategic focus for SAP BTP because of its significant benefits as compared with the Neo environment, including its principles of openness and freedom of choice in terms of support for multiple infrastructure providers, data centers, and a wide range of runtimes and programming languages. 

There are two options to perform the migration based on the requirements. One is SAP provided migration package to migrate the interfaces and other related objects. Other option is to use manual export and import to migrate the objects from Neo to Cloud Foundry.

In this blog I will be using SAP provided Migration pack to migrate the objects from Neo environment to Cloud Foundry.

Every migration project should have following stages:

  1. Discover – In the discovery phase we identify the interfaces, security material, certificates, variables, data store, number ranges, credentials, PGP encryption and other objects which we need to migrate. We prepare the list of interfaces, third parties integrated with SAP, SAP and Non-SAP systems integrated, Cloud or on-premises system integration, Cloud connector configuration etc.
  2. Plan & Prepare – In Plan & Prepare phase we check the readiness of Neo and Cloud Foundry. In Neo environment we should have all artifacts saved in version as a prerequisite. In draft mode automatic migration will not work. We should communicate with third parties connected about the migration plan and inform them about the endpoint changes required. We prepare the target or Cloud Foundry tenant.
  3. Migration – In this step we perform the actual migration process from neo to Cloud foundry. There are certain steps required to prepare for the migration process automatically which I will discuss later.
  4. Validation- Once we complete the migration process, we need to validate. We need to raise the ticket to SAP to validate the migration. Also, we can test the simple iflow as a part of pilot and validate the same.

In this blog I will explain step by step process to migrate from Neo to CF environment using SAP provided Migration pack.

Prerequisites:

  • Provision and setup SAP BTP Integration Suite Cloud Foundry environment.
  • Create and Instances for both plan Integration flow as well as API.
  • Create Service key for both the plan, which we will need for authentication purpose.
  • Assign proper roles to user who is performing migration in Neo as well as in CF tenant.
  • Postman should be installed.
  • Download the compressed file available in SAP note 2937549, This file contains postman collection for migration.

Migration Process:

We perform the migration process by running several Postman requests. To execute these requests, we can use a set of Postman collections predefined by SAP.  First, we need to prepare the environment file. In order to create an environment file, we should setup Oauth authentication in Neo and CF.

  1. Configure Oauth Authentication in Neo environment:

Set Up an Oauth in both NEO (for NEO make sure you generate Oauth on “tmn” node ) and CF (For CF make sure the plan selected is “api” )

Login to BTP Cockpit and Neo subaccount. Goto Securityà Oauth, click on Register new client.

Note down the client ID and Secret, this will be used as user id and password in migration.

Using the client ID from the previous step, navigate to Security->Authorizations and assign the user oauth_client_<client id> to the AuthGroup.IntegrationDeveloper and AuthGroup.Administrator roles.

 

  1. Configure authentication in Cloud Foundry:

Login to Cloud foundry tenant and navigate to the Space, Services->Service Marketplace and search for Process Integration.  Click Create. Create a new Instance of plan type api. Then create a service key for the same instance.

Once Service key is created, we can note down the client id, secret, URLs for later use.

Now we are ready to create environment file for migration process.

Next, download the compressed file from SAP Note 2937549 and extract it to a local folder on your computer.

In the extracted folder, open the file CPI MIG Settings.postman_environment and configure the parameters with the hostnames from the previous steps:

Now we need to update following values in the environment file.

sourceHost, targetHost, sourceTokenHost, targetToeknHost, sourceOauthId, sourceOauthSecret, targetOauthId, targetOauthSecret.

Save the file.
Now launch Postman and Import the folder by choosing:

File->New->Import Folder and select the unpacked folder.

 

 

Make sure you have the correct environment selected so the proper hostnames and credentials are used.

Now open the first collection in postman CPI MIG010 Connects to Tenants and click on run.

Now run CPI MIG020 for readiness check.

We have multiple Postman collection to migrate objects like credentials, Certificates, Variables, data Store, Packages, Oauth to Client credentials, Number range, Value mapping. Now based on our requirement we run these postman collections to migrate the objects from Neo to CF tenant.

To migrate credentials, we will run CPI MIG 070 User Credentials.

All credentials migrated from Neo to CF. Now we need to update passwords manually in Cloud Foundry.

Run MIG080- OAuth credentials

Run CPI MIG030 for variable migration:

Run CPI MIG060 to migrate Custom public certificates:

Run CPI MIG120 to migrate custom content:

 There is a field in environment file "key": "migratePackages", if we want to migrate all the custom packages then we can leave this field value empty, else we can specify the package IDs to be migrated.

We migrated successfully 3 packages which contains multiple Integration Flows from Neo to CF.

Once we migrate the Integration Flows to cloud foundry environment, we need to configure them and deploy them manually. After deployment we need to make a note of endpoints and share with the system that call Cloud Integration. We need to update the firewalls or IP address which are whitelisted in Neo environment to Cloud Foundry.

 Reference:

https://me.sap.com/notes/3351844

2937549 - SAP Cloud Integration for process services: Content Migration from Neo Environment to Multi-Cloud Foundation - SAP for Me

Migrating SAP Cloud Integration to SAP Integration Suite | SAP Help Portal

 

Thanks & Regards,

Nisha Shrivansh

entry.title: Customizing Automatic Notification Creation in T350 for Specific Plants
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are facing a challenge with customizing the automatic creation of notifications in T350 for different plants. Specifically, we need notifications to be automatically created when creating orders for plants 1900, 3100, and 3200, but not for other plants.

Our initial approach was to generally enable the creation of notifications and then modify this behavior using the enhancements ZI_WISE_NOTI_T350 and ZI_WISE_NOTI_T350_IW32. Unfortunately, we encountered an issue where the notification title is not utilized. Additionally, this method does not work when an order is created using a maintenance plan via transaction IP10, as the function module LIPM5F13 is called, and there are no enhancement spots available in this context.

Given these constraints, we are seeking advice on how to achieve the desired result. Is there a way to customize the automatic creation of notifications for specific plants when creating orders, including those generated through maintenance plans?

Any guidance or suggestions on how to approach this problem would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you in advance for your help!

Best regards,

entry.title: Augmentation Operation in RAP
entry.content:
Ever wondered how to set default values for the object page fields during Create Operation in Fiori using RAP at the projection level?

With an augmentation implementation you can add data or modify incoming requests on the projection layer before data reaches the transactional buffer. You can add data to a modify request or issue additional requests by augmenting the operation before the request is passed to the base business object.

Use case

• Defaulting for incoming requests. For example: Populate default values for fields of object page on create operation in Fiori

• Behavior-enabling denormalized fields, for example enabling editing of language dependent fields.

Definition

Augmentation is defined in the projection behavior definition on the relevant operation with the following syntax: 

define behavior for Entity [alias AliasedName]
  ..
{
  use create (augment);
  use update (augment); 

  use association AssocName { create (augment); }
  ...
}

Below steps will result you how to populate default values for the object page fields on create operation especially at project layer.  That means this default values population is specific to projection not for entire BO. 

  1. In your projection behavior ZC_TRAVEL_PROCESSOR_M_XX definition, define augmentation for the create operation.
use create (augment);

2. Add behavior implementation class to the project behavior.

…
define behavior for ZC_TRAVEL_PROCESSOR_M_XX alias Travel
implementation in class zcl_bp_c_travelm_xx
….​

3. Click the activation button or use the shortcut Ctrl + F3 to activate the behavior definition.

4. The ADT Quick Fix feature can be used to create implementation class for travel entity. 

For this, set the cursor on the class name, and press Ctrl+1 to star the Quick Fix dialog.

 Click on create behavior implementation to create the class.

Click Next and Finish to create the class. In Local Types of Class you will see a augmentation operation method created where you need to implement logic to populate default values.

5.In the method implementation paste the below code. 

METHOD augment_create.
  
   DATA: travel_create TYPE TABLE FOR CREATE zi_travel_m_xx.

    travel_create = CORRESPONDING #( entities ).
    LOOP AT travel_create ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<travel>).
      <travel>-agency_id = '070012'.
      <travel>-status  = 'O'.
      <travel>-%control-agency_id = if_abap_behv=>mk-on.
      <travel>-%control-status = if_abap_behv=>mk-on.
    ENDLOOP.

    MODIFY AUGMENTING ENTITIES OF zi_travel_m_xx ENTITY Travel CREATE FROM travel_create.
  ENDMETHOD.

"MODIFY AUGMENTING ENTITIES"  is a EML statement with which you can modify entities of base business object. In the above example "ZI_TRAVEL_M_XX" is the base business object and "ZC_TRAVEL_PROCESSOR_M_XX" is the project business object.

6. Save and Activate the Class.

7.Test the app now using preview tool. On Click of Create Agency ID and Status fields are defaulted.

Same behavior can be achieved with determination as well. But what is the difference ?

entry.title: How to Set Up Quota Arrangement in SAP – A Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
Quota Arrangement is a strategic approach to ensure fair and efficient vendor allocation, helping teams meet objectives without overburdening any single unit. Whether you're managing sales teams, production lines, or project workloads, a well-structured quota system can enhance productivity and accountability.

In this post, we’ll explore a simple yet effective Quota Arrangement Process to help you distribute quotas logically and transparently.

Pre-requisite Configuration:

1. Splitting must be set to Active in the Lot-Sizing Procedure

Path: SPRO > Material Management > Consumption Based Planning > Planning > Lot-Size Calculation > Define Lot-Sizing Procedure > MRP Lot Size

2. Activation of Scope-Dependent Application Job Catalogue Entries

Path: SPRO > ABAP Platform > Application Server > System Administration > Activation of Scope-Dependent Application Job Catalogue Entries

Quota Arrangement Process

Step 1: Quota Arrangement Usage & Source List must be active in Material Master (Transaction: MM01/MM02)

Step 2: Maintain Info Records for the Material/Vendor Combination (Transaction: ME11)

Step 3: Maintain Source List (Transaction: ME01)

Step 4: Create Quota Arrangement (App: Manage Quota Arrangements)

Plant: 1000

Storage Location: 1004

Vendor: 11030, 11031

Mention Material, Plant, Validity, and Minimum Splitting Quantity

Minimum Splitting Qty: Minimum quantity that a lot must have in order to be apportioned among several sources of supply in the planning run.

Create Quota Items for suppliers for Standard Procurement.

Based on the business requirement, you can choose Special Procurement types.

Quota: Enter the portion of the material requirement that is to be procured from the source. The value is automatically converted into a percentage.

                         (Quota base quantity + Quota-allocated quantity)
Quota Rating =  ---------------------------------------------------------------
                                                Quota

This quota rating is calculated for all sources of supply included in the quota arrangement. The source selected is the one with the lowest rating.

Quota Arrangement 11 created

Step 5: Create Purchase Requisition (App: Manage Purchase Requisitions - Professional)

Enter Description, select Document Type, and check for Automatic Source Determination

Create PR Line Items

Enter Plant, Material

Enter Quantity

Under Source of Supply, source will be automatically assigned.

Enter Purchasing Group

PR Created 100000060

Step 6: Check Quota Arrangements App (App: Manage Quota Arrangements)

Since PR was created for 1.000 BAG and automatically assigned for the 11030 vendor, the Quota Allocated Qty gets updated.

Step 7: Repeat PR Creation Process (App: Manage Purchase Requisition - Professional)

Refer to Step 5 for detailed steps for PR creation 

PR Created 100000061

Step 8: Check Quota Arrangements App (App: Manage Quota Arrangements)

Since PR's were created for 1.000 BAG and 1.500 and automatically assigned for the 11030 vendor, the Quota Allocated Qty gets updated.

Step 9: Schedule MRP Runs (App: Schedules MRP Runs or Transaction: MD02)

Before running MRP, let's check the quota arrangement preview

Navigate to the Schedule MRP Run app

If pre-requisite config settings are maintained, the Job Template will appear here.

Navigate to Step 2 and click "Define Recurrence Pattern"

Navigate to Step 3, enter parameters like Plant, Material and check for Changed BOM Components and All order BOM components.

MRP Scheduled and Finished

Step 10: Monitor Stock/Requirement List

Enter Material, Plant

Two Purchase Requisitions created as per the Quota mentioned;

Total Requirement = 9.000 BAG

For supplier 11030, quota 60%

60% of 9.000 is 5.400 BAG

For supplier 11031, quota 40%

40% of 9.000 is 3.600 BAG

Step 11: Check Quota Arrangements App (App: Manage Quota Arrangements)

The allocated quantity is updated under the respective quota arrangements items for the respective suppliers.

Step 12: Create Purchase Requisition (App: Manage Purchase Requisitions - Professional)

Refer to Step 5 for detailed steps for PR creation

Quota Rating for supplier 11030

                         (Quota base quantity + Quota-allocated quantity)
Quota Rating =  ---------------------------------------------------------------
                                                Quota

Quota Rating = 5.400/60 

                     = 90

Quota Rating for supplier 11031

                         (Quota base quantity + Quota-allocated quantity)
Quota Rating =  ---------------------------------------------------------------
                                                Quota

Quota Rating = 3.600/40 

                     = 90

Based on quota is calculated. The lowest rating source gets assigned to any further requirements that are created.

PR Created 100000062

 

Ref. blogs

Assembly Subcontracting Process in Production Planning - SAP S/4 HANA

Material Staging in Production Supply Area-WM/PP Integration

Material Staging through MF60 - WM to IM Managed Location

 

Regards,

Leelamohan

entry.title: Customer Master Data - Extension
entry.content:
Hi,

For customer extension, it is mandatory that the customer master data already exists in the system — this means you can only perform the extension after the customer master has been successfully uploaded.

Separate Template for Extension:
A dedicated template is used for customer master data extension — this is different from the normal creation template, given below :

  • Customer - extend existing record by new org levels

(Here we are talking with the scenario)

Run ID is Critical:
A unique Run ID is assigned for each extension. If Customer A is being extended to 2 sales areas, then two separate Run IDs will be used, e.g.:

  • Sales Area 1: Run ID = 1

  • Sales Area 2: Run ID = 2

Sequential Processing Required:
Extension uploads must be done sequentially:
First, the uploader for Run ID 1 will be executed (for Sales Area 1),
Then, the uploader for Run ID 2 will be executed (for Sales Area 2).

Separate Uploaders for Each Run ID:
Multiple sales area extensions are not allowed in a single uploader. The system does not accept so many changes in one uploader, therefore, each Run ID must be processed in a separate uploader.

Customer Must Exist in the System:
Before performing any extension, it is mandatory that the customer master data already exists in the system. This means the extension can only be carried out after the initial customer creation/upload has been successfully completed.

Best regards,

Tehreem Fatima

entry.title: SAP Community Paris Meetup #8 - Recap & attendance record 🚀!
entry.content:
On April 8th, we had the pleasure of gathering for a new SAP Community Paris meetup, and what a night it was, just EPIC !!
Thanks to the amazing parisian energy, we hit a record: over 110 participants joined us at Malt’s beautiful office in Paris. It was great to see so many familiar faces, and lots of new ones too!

VINCI Energies: 7 Years on S/4HANA: Lessons and Next Steps

The evening started with a very interesting session from the team at VINCI Energies, who shared their experience with S/4HANA.

They made the move 7 years ago, well ahead of the curve, and gave us a deep, honest look into their experience:

  • Why they decided to migrate early
  • The challenges they faced (both technical and human)
  • What they’ve learned across multiple areas: finance, logistics, development, and architecture
  • And how their transformation is still evolving today

It was an honest and practical presentation, full of real-life examples and tips for anyone preparing or going through a migration.

SAP: What’s new and what’s coming

Next, the team from SAP France gave us a look at the latest updates in the SAP world.
They covered some exciting topics:

  • New features in finance, like DRC
  • The evolution of GROW and RISE
  • Real-world use cases for AI, BTP, and Business Data Cloud

There was a ton of interest, and lots of great questions from the audience — proof that the community is eager to learn and ready to move forward.

Big thanks to everyone!

This event was a success thanks to so many people:

  • VINCI Energies, for their transparency and valuable feedback
  • SAP France,for their clear and inspiring session
  • Malt, for being such wonderful hosts (and for the drinks and food too!)
  • Espresso Tutorials, for the great ebook giveaways 📚
  • And most of all: YOU, the community, for being there, sharing your energy, and making SAP Community Paris what it is today 💙

What’s next?

We’re already working on the next events, and trust us, you won’t want to miss them.
See you soon. And until then, keep learning, keep sharing, and keep the spirit of community alive! 🔥

entry.title: Re: Offline Capabilities and Best Approach for Mobile App...
entry.content:
@DavidAvila 

The solution you're looking to develop closely resembles an existing offering from SAPSAP Service & Asset Manager (SSAM). I would recommend evaluating this application to see if this meets your requirements. SSAM app is built using the on the Mobile Development Kit (MDK).

SAP provides various front-end developments options for mobile applications through SAP Mobile Services, including: MDK, SAP BTP SDK for Android, and SAP BTP SDK for iOS. The choice between them often depends on your team's technical skillset and specific business needs. See some note around this here.

All of these SDKs support offline capabilities, which is essential for your use case involving field operations in areas with limited connectivity.  You can find out the details in this help documentation.

entry.title: Activate Notifications for Market Data Request background job
entry.content:
Situation handling framework has notification function to enable the system to send a job log once the job run is completed in the notification area on the SAP Fiori launchpad and email.

To integrate with situation handling, Treasury and Risk Management provides the following:

  • Situation Template: FIN_TRM_REQUEST_MARKET_DATA
  • Standard Team Category: FSCM_TRM

Here are the steps to start the configuration of Situation Template: Market Data Request to receive notifications about this job.

Step 1: Start with creating Teams and Responsibilities.

  1. Open App 'Manage Teams and Responsibilities' (F2412).
  2. Choose Create.
  3. In the General Information enter the data:
    Name: ex Z_TEST_MD_01
    Type: STRM_MD
    Description: Test description for situation handling
    Status: Enable
  4. In the Team members section press 'Create' and maintain:
    Business Partner: Choose the users to get the notification in F4 help.
    Functions: STRM_MD_AD.
  5. Choose Save

Step 2: Enable Situation Types

  1. Open App 'Manage Situation Types - Extended' (F5437).
  2. Click the Create button to select ‘Create Situation Type’.
  3. On the Create New Situation Type screen enter:
    ID: Test_TRM_MD_ST01
    Name: Market Data Request
    Description: Market Data Request notification
    Situation Scenario: FIN_TRM_MD_REQUEST_MSG_MONITOR
    Situation Template: FIN_TRM_REQUEST_MARKET_DATA
    Press Create button.
  4. On the Header Information for Situation types:
    Status: Enabled
    In the Recipients > Responsibility by Teams > Team Category: 'FSCM_TRM'
  5.  Filter by Member Function: Administration Employee for Market Data.
  6. In the Message-Based Triggers section, check the following data is in assigned:
    Item 1 : Market Data Request Failed
    Item 2 : Market Data Request Successful
    Item 3 : Market Data Req. Successful with Warning
  7. Select items from the Message-Based Triggers section and navigate to the Situation Display to insert variables for detailed notifications and emails. Ensure default Situation Texts are assigned
  8. Choose Create.

Step 3: User Setting for Notification

  1. Log on to the SAP Fiori launchpad.
  2. Open profile of the logged-on user in the right corner
  3. Choose Notifications in Settings.
  4. On the Notifications screen, specify the following and select Save
    Email: select
    High Priority: check
    Enable: Enabled

Step 4: Schedule a Background Job to Trigger the Notifications

  1. Use App Schedule Treasury Back Office Jobs (F2771).
  2. Schedule the job template 'Request Current Market Data'.

Step 5: Check the Notification in Fiori Launchpad and E-Mail.

 

Reference:-

Situation Template: Market Data Request

entry.title: Re: Issue with Email Notification Link for background job...
entry.content:
The link from email notification displays error due to the missing role for App My Situations (F4537).

Follow the steps given in the blog post to configure email notification using situation handling for background jobs.

Activate Notifications for Market Data Request background job

Link in the email notification works after assigning your user with the business role ex 'BR_TREASURY_SPECIALIST_BOE' that contains the business catalog 'SAP_CA_BC_SITN_MYSITUATION_PC' (Situation Handling - My Situations).

Refer SAP KBA 3598175 - Issue with Email Notification Link for background job

entry.title: Re: Issue with Determining Current Day of the Week in CDS View Calculation Field
entry.content:
Hi -

is this for Developer Extensibility or Key User (like in a BAdI)? some of the operators you are using are not in the proper format for Restricted ABAP

for example mod should be like

lv_numberI = 15 mod 4.  

not MOD(15,4)

DATS_DAYS_BETWEEN() does not seem to be available either.

What kind of inconsistent results are you getting?

Thank you
Jerry

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud – Need APIs for Automating Finance Data Uploads (No Manual Entry)
entry.content:
Dear SAP Experts,

We are implementing SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud for our client, and the business has a strict requirement for automated data uploads in the Finance module — they do not accept manual entries for the scenarios listed below.

We explored available options like Migration Cockpit, SOAP/REST APIs, and standard CDS views, but we could not identify suitable APIs or methods for the following requirements.

Module Business Requirement Current Situation Our Consulting Concern
FinanceVendor invoice uploadManual entry via FIORINo API found; how to upload invoices automatically when HSN Code is missing?
FinanceCustomer invoice uploadManual entry via FIORIIs there an API or method to upload customer invoices without HSN Code?
FinanceCustomer to Customer transfer postingManualCan this be automated using Migration Cockpit or an API in Public Cloud?
FinanceVendor to Vendor transfer postingManualAny way to automate via standard APIs in S/4HANA Public Cloud?
FinanceGL Account creationManual copyCan GL master data be created via API/Migration Object in Public Cloud?
FinanceDepreciation Key creationManual configHow can we create a new depreciation key (different rate) via automated process or API?
FinanceGL Line Item ChangeManualIs there a way to update GL line items automatically (via API or standard FIORI)?
FinanceDelivery Report UploadNot supportedIs there a way to upload delivery-related reports or data in Public Cloud?

➤ Our Expectations:

  • API endpoints (if available) to handle these operations

  • Workarounds or alternative integration strategies (e.g., iRPA, SAP BTP, Integration Suite)

  • Guidance on best practices for Public Cloud automation (data load without user intervention)

Any documentation, API hub links, or practical insights from similar implementations would be very helpful.

entry.title: No Rounding rule should happen for a Condition type
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

We have a pricing-related requirement in our project. While reviewing the condition type settings, I noticed that there are three available options for the rounding rule. However, in our case, we do not want to apply any rounding. Below are the rounding rules in SAP.

 

Is there a way to deactivate or bypass the rounding rule completely?

Requirement Details:

  • We are using manual pricing conditions.

  • The price is received from a non-SAP system.

  • For example, if the incoming price is 4.567, SAP should store it as 4.56, not 4.57.

Looking forward to your guidance on how this can be achieved.

entry.title: cds.test using jest and axios not working for POST request
entry.content:
I have written a unit test where I am trying to test a CREATE API which vaildates the data and increments the material number. 

this is the service defintion

 

service MaterialService {
  entity Materials as projection on db.Materials;
}

 

this is the service implementation

 

export class MaterialService extends ApplicationService {

  init() {

    this.before("POST", "Materials", async (req: Request) => this.validateMaterial(req))

    return super.init()

  }

  async validateMaterial(req: Request) {
    let material: Material = req.data

    const { Materials } = this.entities

    // Validate Material Type

    if (material.type != "ABC" && material.type != "XYZ") {
      req.error({
        code: 500,
        message: "Invalid Material Type"
      })
    }

    // generate a material number, +1 the last matnr

    let latestMaterialNumber = await SELECT.one.from(Materials).orderBy(`matnr desc`)

    if (latestMaterialNumber) {
      material.matnr = latestMaterialNumber.matnr + 1
    } else {
      material.matnr = 1
    }

    req.data = material

    return req

  }

}

 

and, this is the test

 

describe(('material-service-test'), () => {
  const { POST } = cds.test(__dirname + '/..')

  it('should create material successfully', async () => {

    const material = {
      name: "Samsung Galaxy S24",
      description: "Smartphone",
      type: "ABCD"
    }

    const response = await POST('/odata/v4/material/Materials', material)
    console.log(response)
})

 

I get the following error message, which indicates that the this.before() hook is not being called while making the POST request

 

  material-service-test
    × should create material successfully (381 ms)                                                                    
                                                                                                                      
  ● material-service-test › should create material successfully                                                       
                                                                                                                      
    SQLITE_CONSTRAINT_NOTNULL - NOT NULL constraint failed: db_Materials.matnr                                        
                                                                                                                      
      29 |     }                                                                                                      
      30 |                                                                                                            
    > 31 |     const response = await POST('/odata/v4/material/Materials', material)
         |                      ^
      32 |
      33 |     console.log(response)
      34 |

      at Object.<anonymous> (test/material-service.test.js:31:22)

    Cause:
    NOT NULL constraint failed: db_Materials.matnr

      29 |     }
      30 |
    > 31 |     const response = await POST('/odata/v4/material/Materials', material)
         |                      ^
      32 |
      33 |     console.log(response)
      34 |

      at _error (node_modules/@cap-js/cds-test/lib/axios.js:59:55)
      at Object.<anonymous> (test/material-service.test.js:31:22)

 

How can I fix this issue?

Thanks

entry.title: Nested Post Calls in SAP Build Apps
entry.content:
Hi all,

I’ve been working on an interesting use case where I’m creating a product catalog page that allows users to view individual product details pages when selecting a product. As part of this functionality, I’m leveraging a standard service to post data into two entities: A_SalesOrder and A_SalesOrderItem. Here's what I’ve encountered so far and where I need help!

 



What I’m Trying to Achieve

  1. Create a Sales Order Record: Populate the header fields (e.g., Sales Order Type, Sales Order, Sales Organization, Distribution Channel, etc.) in the A_SalesOrder entity.

  2. Add Line Items to the Sales Order: Populate the details (e.g., Item Category, Material, Quantity, Price, etc.) in the A_SalesOrderItem entity.

  3. Link Header and Line Items: Ensure that the created SalesOrder is used to associate line items correctly.

    sharing the payload I am following for the post request:

    {
      "d": {
        "SalesOrderType": "OR",
        "SalesOrganization": "1710",
        "DistributionChannel": "10",
        "OrganizationDivision": "00",
        "SoldToParty": "17100100",
        "to_Item": {
          "results": [
            {
              "SalesOrderItem": "000010",
              "SalesOrderItemCategory": "TAN",
              "SalesOrderItemText": "Trad.Good 11,PD,Reg.Trading",
              "Material": "TG11",
              "RequestedQuantity": "10",
              "RequestedQuantityUnit": "PC",
              "NetAmount": "175"

              }
          ]
    }
      }
    }

    I am able to successfully post to the header fields using A_SalesOrder. However, when attempting to post data to the line items (A_SalesOrderItem), I encounter a 400 error (Bad Request) and the post fails.

    this is while I am posting to the header fields.


    while doing the post call to the line items I face this. ( The 941 refers to the salesOrder number while doing the post call to the A_SalesOrder service.)


      • Tried associating the SalesOrder ID with the to_Item nested structure and posting to A_SalesOrderItem using an update record action.

      • Attempted to post to both entities (A_SalesOrder and A_SalesOrderItem) in a single nested post call.

    Despite these efforts, I wasn’t able to achieve the desired result, and the 400 error persisted while posting to the line items.

     

    - I’d appreciate guidance on how to make a nested post call that successfully links the A_SalesOrder header and A_SalesOrderItem line items. Specifically:

    1. How to structure the request payload correctly for a nested post.

    2. Whether there are specific settings or configurations required in the service or app variables for associating these entities.

    3. Any example implementations or best practices for handling such scenarios in SAP Build Apps.

    Looking forward to your insights and expertise to resolve this! 😊

     

    @Dan_Wroblewski 
    @eshrinivasan 
    @Joni 

    SAP Build Apps

entry.title: What’s New in SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting : 1H 2025
entry.content:
The 1H 2025 release for SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting brings several enhancements designed to improve recruiter efficiency, streamline the candidate experience, and prepare customers for long-term innovation such as AI integration and the Unified Data Model. Let’s deep dive into the release highlights and see how they can help you optimize your recruiting processes.

Key Highlights at a Glance

I would like to start with a quick overview of the key highlights in Recruiting for 1H 2025 before we dive deeper. Listed all the features and description in a tabular format. 

Feature/Enhancement & Description: 

Reimagined Candidate ExperienceA streamlined, modern UI for internal and external candidates to improve application completion rates.
Drag-and-Drop in Applicant ManagementEasily move candidates across pipeline stages with new drag-and-drop interactions.
AI-Assisted Writing ExpansionAvailable across all Rich Text Editor fields like job descriptions, offer letters, and emails.
New Components for Career Site BuilderSimilar Jobs and Upload Resume components improve discoverability and engagement.
Enhanced Story ReportsOperators can now access job requisition skill reports and audit logs.
Updated Job Requisition Details PageRemoval of the Job Profile tab to align with standard UDM fields.
Improved Authorization for Business RulesBetter permission granularity when configuring recruiting-related business rules.

 

Major Enhancements in Detail

1. Reimagined Candidate Experience

A completely redesigned flow for both internal and external applicants, structured around:

  • Guided Application: Step-by-step application with contextual guidance.
  • My Applications: Easy tracking of current applications.
  • My Profile and Manage Account: Improved control over candidate profile information.

Configuration: Enable Candidate Experience for Career Site from Admin Center > Manage Recruiting Settings  > Career Site

Why it matters: Reduces drop-off rates and makes applying easier than ever.

⚠️Note: I encountered the Error Code: 404 - Page Not Found multiple times. If you've experienced the same, feel free to comment below and share how you resolved it.

2. Drag-and-Drop in Applicant Management

Recruiters can now visually move candidates through the hiring process using drag-and-drop functionality. Business rules and permissions are fully respected.

Key Benefits:
✔ Single or bulk movement of applicants.
✔ Automatic permission checks to ensure compliance.
✔ Clear error feedback if actions aren’t allowed.

Why it matters: Saves time and makes pipeline management more intuitive.

3. AI-Assisted Writing in More Areas

AI-generated text suggestions now appear in:

  • Job descriptions
  • Interview invitations
  • Offer letters
  • Candidate emails
  • Agency comments

 Image Source: SAP

Why it matters: Enhances consistency, professionalism, and writing speed.

4. New Career Site Components

  • Upload Resume Component: Lets job seekers upload their resume to find relevant jobs.

Image Source: SAP

  • Similar Jobs Component: Displays related jobs on the job detail page.

Image Source: SAP 

How It Works?

  • Displays job cards based on skills and location.

  • No extra setup needed if AI-assisted skills matching is enabled.

Why it matters: Improves job discovery and reduces bounce rates.

5. Enhanced Story Reports Access

Recruiting operators can now access audit and job skill reports directly through Story Reports.

Why it matters: Enables data-driven hiring decisions with real-time visibility.

6. Enhanced Application Data Download

Recruiters can now download all candidate documents (resumes, cover letters, attachments) as a single PDF or ZIP file.

Features:
✔ Available for one or multiple applicants.
✔ Files remain accessible for 24 hours before auto-deletion.
✔ Email notifications when downloads are ready.

Why it matters: Simplifies interview preparation and compliance audits.

7. Standard Site URLs for Career Sites

Organizations can now use SAP-hosted URLs (e.g., mycompany.jobs.hr.cloud.sap) to avoid managing DNS/SSL.

Transition Process:

  • Run both custom and standard URLs for up to 6 months.

  • Automatically updates job alerts and integrations

Why it matters: Reduces IT maintenance efforts.

⚠️ Note: The 6-month transition begins when you change the Primary URL. Related settings update automatically. After 6 months, the old URL is removed and stops redirecting.

 
8. Additional Technical Enhancements
  • Download applicant documents (resume, cover letter) as PDF or ZIP.
  • Skills now automatically migrate when copying a job requisition.
  • The Job Profile tab has been removed to align with the Unified Data Model (UDM).
  • Salary fields support more digits for high-compensation roles.
  • New granularity for permissions tied to recruiting-related business rules improves control and governance.
  • Interview scheduling configuration migrated to Work Tech Configuration.
  • Updated banner notifications and iconography in the new Applicant Management tool.

⚠️Deprecations to Note

Feature & Status:

Spell CheckEnd of Maintenance: May 17, 2024. Deleted: Nov 14, 2025.
"Top Jobs" and "View All Jobs" LinksEnd of Maintenance: Nov 15, 2024. Deleted: May 15, 2026.

 

Final Thoughts

The 1H 2025 release for SAP SuccessFactors Recruiting introduces meaningful enhancements across the candidate experience, recruiter tools, reporting, and integration capabilities. From AI-assisted writing to streamlined pipeline management and career site improvements, these updates are designed to drive smarter, faster, and more engaging hiring processes.

Take time to explore these features in your preview environment, adjust configurations as needed, and collaborate with your recruiting teams to ensure a smooth rollout and optimal impact.

For full technical documentation, visit the SAP SuccessFactors What's New Viewer

entry.title: About Solution Manager monitoring settings
entry.content:
Dear SAP,

I want to change the monitoring settings in Solution Manager for a metric.

I want to modify the threshold settings to skip yellow and work only from green to red so that errors can be detected immediately.

What settings do I need to change from green to red?


If it is not possible to skip yellow and set green to red, What settings are possible to detect similar errors?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: About Solution Manager monitoring settings
entry.content:
You need to create a custom template in order to edit metrics.

##2506790 - How to Create a Custom Template to change metrics, thresholds or alerts in a SAP standard template - SAP Solution Manager

To modify custom metrics, refer to Support Portal documentation:

System Monitoring

Create Custom Metrics

Custom metric is supported in case the SAP delivered standard metrics could not satisfy the user needs. This section explains how you can create a custom metric in a custom template.

  • Navigate to a custom template
  • Change to Expert Mode 
  • Press the button Create and select Metric from the drop-down list. Afterwards, the Custom Metric Creation Wizard shows up at the right hand side. The Custom Metric Creation Wizard consists of two major steps, namely Specify Metric Attributes and Assignments. In the first step Specify Metric Attributes, the user should enter the details of metrics such as metric name and type and, more importantly, determine how the data can be collected (data provider, parameter, collection interval etc.).Then in the step Assignments, the user needs to specify the alert, to which this custom metric is assigned. The custom metric can be either assigned to a already existing standard alert or also to a custom alert. 
  • Fill in the name, category, class, data type and technical name for the custom metric under the Overview tab. The user can give a meaningful name to the custom metric and select a category out of the pre-defined categories Availability, Performance, Exceptions, Configuration and Self-Monitoring. The first four categories are self-explained and the Self-Monitoring category deals with the monitoring of SAP Solution Manager itself such as Monitoring and Alerting Infrastructure (MAI) and Extractor Framework. Next, the user can specify the class as either Metric or Metric Group, depending on whether the data provider returns more than one value. The data type is used as usual and can be selected among String, Floating Point and Integer. It is also required to enter a technical name, with which the metric can be checked easily, for instance, in the Alerting Directory. Lastly, do not forget to check the checkbox Active so that the metric can be used in the next step.
  • Select the required data collector from the list of provided data collectors and enter the parameter value. In this example, it is intended to search any warning messages in the dev_icm log file. More details about the usage of data collector File Text Pattern Search can be found in SAP Note 2257249.
  • Define the threshold for alerting
  • Assign the custom metric to a alert. Since in this example, we want to assign this custom metric to a custom alert later on, the assignment here is left empty.
    Lastly, click Finish button to complete creating the custom metric.
  • Do not forget to press Save button to persist the changes made in the custom template before going further. Consequently, the newly created custom metric can be found in the table of metrics under the tab Metrics. It is easy to find out that the checkbox under the column Custom-Created is checked, which indicates that this metric is not SAP delivered standard metric but a custom-made one. 

About which threshold type you need to use, we recommend Numeric Threshold (Green/Red)

Refer to 2491414 - Details of Threshold types for metrics in System Monitoring

entry.title: Re: HOW TO RETRIVE more than 2000 records from salesforce...
entry.content:
Hello @Saikumar_P ,

The Salesforce adapter can return a maximum of 2000 records per call — this is a limit and cannot be exceeded in a single request.
To retrieve more than 2000 records, you can either enable the "Auto Pagination" option available within the adapter configuration, or implement custom pagination, as demonstrated in this blog.

Seamless Integration with SAP CI: Mastering Pagination for Salesforce Adapter 

Let me know if this helps.

 

Thanks, 

Punith Oswal

entry.title: Re: Email Trigger Issue with OAuth2Password Authenticatio...
entry.content:
Hi @gregorw, Thanks for the response.

The below code was working fine for BasicAuth:

const { sendMail } = require('@sap-cloud-sdk/mail-client');

module.exports = srv => {
srv.on("sendmail", async (req) => {
const { to, subject, text } = req.data;
const mailConfig = {
from: 'example@sap.com',
to: to,
subject: subject,
text: text
};

try {
await sendMail({ destinationName: 'sap_process_automation_mail' }, [mailConfig]);
return 'Email sent successfully.';
} catch (error) {
console.log(error);
return 'Something went wrong!';
}
});
};

 

When I updated the authentication type to OAuth2Password, it started throwing error: The destination 'name: sap_process_automation_mail' does not contain host or port information as properties. Please configure in the "Additional Properties" of the destination. 

Also, I'm able to trigger emails using BPA>Control Tower(OAuth2Password destination).

entry.title: Eclipse ADT: impossible to add a breakpoint in ABAP source but no explanation (max 30)
entry.content:
I'd like your opinion about the below issue.

To reproduce it:

  1. Remove all breakpoints in the current system
  2. Create ABAP program with 31 lines ASSERT 1 = 1 (or any neutral ABAP line you want)
  3. Add a breakpoint on each line
  4. There's an error "The maximum number of breakpoints is 30" when adding the 31st one:

     

  5. Click "Do not show again".
  6. Add breakpoints again to reach 31.
  7. You can't add a 31st breakpoint but no error is shown.

Of course, one would argue that it does what you asked ("do not show again") but my point is that the error (the user action is refused) should not be considered like a warning, and also because reaching the limit of 30 breakpoints happens rarely and you may not remember that you had checked "do not show again". My opinion is that the checkbox "Do not show again" should not be proposed and the error should always be shown.

To get the error message back, go to Preferences > ABAP Development > Debug > Message Settings > Select "Show message if breakpoint cannot be set":

What do you think? (*)

Thank you.

Sandra

My system: ABAP 7.52 SP04, ADT 3.48, Eclipse 2025-03.

CC @Armin_Beil, I'd appreciate your view or plan on this.

(*) NB: there doesn't seem to be an area in "influence SAP" for ABAP/ADT stuff, so I'm posting the question/request here.
